mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a p-value below 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During rate hiking cycles, declining industrial production reflects tightening credit constraints on manufacturers, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles show no significant negative return differential versus baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above threshold consistently precede positive market returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE trends show no predictive power for market direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, surging housing starts signal overheating and impending monetary policy correction, resulting in negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST spikes in late-cycle periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance.
